rationale.R1.2: The paragraph contains at least one apparent non-sequitur (Cost Auditor filters expensive plans → therefore queries are more readable to DBAs) and a secondary thesis about documentation mining whose logical connection to the primary contribution is asserted but not demonstrated; scrutinising logical consistency is a plausible primary goal of 'take a look'.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple quantitative claims are made (71.4 % vs 64.2 % execution accuracy; 68 % description agreement; 4.1/5 DBA rating from a 'small' user study) without visible methodological grounding; checking whether claims are adequately supported by the reported evidence is a natural read-through responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' is a casual, open-ended instruction that most commonly signals a request for general prose feedback; sentence- and paragraph-level writing quality is therefore a plausible load-bearing interpretation.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on (a) logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment — scrutinising whether the stated conclusions follow from the premises and are supported by the reported numbers — or (b) writing polish — improving clarity, flow, and prose quality of the passage?
